examples.text:
أَغَايَةُ الدِّينِ أَنْ تُحْفُوا شَوَارِبَكُمْ
يَا أُمَّةً ضَحِكَتْ مِنْ جَهْلِهَا الْأُمَمُ
ʔaḡāyatu d-dīni ʔan tuḥfū šawāribakum
examples.english: Is it the purpose of religion that you should trim your moustaches? / What a nation at whose ignorance other nations laugh!

etymology.text: Derived from the active participle of the verb شَرِبَ (šariba, “to drink”).
